task_completion.description: The androidx.biometric dependency is added to the app's build.gradle.kts file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the app/build.gradle.kts file open in an editor, showing a line like implementation("androidx.biometric:biometric:1.2.0-alpha05") in the dependencies block.

task_completion.description: A BiometricPrompt instance is created with an AuthenticationCallback.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for LoginActivity.kt open in an editor, showing the initialization of a BiometricPrompt object that includes an object : BiometricPrompt.AuthenticationCallback() with overridden methods like onAuthenticationSucceeded.

task_completion.description: PromptInfo is configured with the correct title and negative button text.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for LoginActivity.kt showing BiometricPrompt.PromptInfo.Builder() with .setTitle("Biometric Login") and .setNegativeButtonText("Cancel").

task_completion.description: The login button click listener is updated to trigger the biometric prompt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for LoginActivity.kt showing the login button's setOnClickListener block calling .authenticate(promptInfo) on the BiometricPrompt instance.

task_completion.description: Navigation to DiaryActivity is moved inside the successful authentication callback.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for LoginActivity.kt showing the startActivity(Intent(this, DiaryActivity::class.java)) code placed strictly inside the onAuthenticationSucceeded method of the AuthenticationCallback, rather than directly inside the button's click listener.

integrity.description: The agent used an IDE or text editor to modify the files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show Android Studio or another code editor being used to edit the Kotlin and Gradle files, rather than terminal commands like 'echo' or 'sed' doing all the work.

integrity.description: The agent did not mock or bypass the biometric authentication flow.
integrity.visual_evidence: The code should show genuine usage of the AndroidX BiometricPrompt API. The onAuthenticationSucceeded method should not be called manually from the button click listener.

integrity.description: The modifications were made to the correct files in the SecretDiary project.
integrity.visual_evidence: The editor tabs or file tree should confirm that the edited files are app/build.gradle.kts and app/src/main/java/com/example/secretdiary/LoginActivity.kt within the SecretDiary project directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project is located at /home/ga/AndroidStudioProjects/SecretDiary. The target files are app/build.gradle.kts and app/src/main/java/com/example/secretdiary/LoginActivity.kt. The required dependency is exactly 'androidx.biometric:biometric' (version 1.2.0-alpha05). The PromptInfo must be configured with .setTitle("Biometric Login") and .setNegativeButtonText("Cancel"). Crucially, the intent to start DiaryActivity must be moved from the button's onClick listener into the BiometricPrompt.AuthenticationCallback's onAuthenticationSucceeded method.
